introSection: Gravity Roller Coaster is a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Miler Manufacturing located at Schnepf Farms in Queen Creek, Arizona, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Gravity Roller Coaster was built in 1953 by Miler Manufacturing, and eventually made its way to Schnepf Farms on October 6, 2007. The ride originally opened as Peachtree Express until 2012 where it was renamed to Gravity Roller Coaster.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride had an oval layout with a lift hill and two small bunny hills. The track and supports are gray.

fullText:
Gravity Roller Coaster is a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Miler Manufacturing located at Schnepf Farms in Queen Creek, Arizona, USA.

Gravity Roller Coaster was built in 1953 by Miler Manufacturing, and eventually made its way to Schnepf Farms on October 6, 2007. The ride originally opened as Peachtree Express until 2012 where it was renamed to Gravity Roller Coaster.

The ride had an oval layout with a lift hill and two small bunny hills. The track and supports are gray.
